{"society_power": 5.5, "state_power": 4.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Confessional consolidation, merchant republic, and 1952 Rosewater Revolution", "reasoning": "The mercantile boom and post-customs-union revenue modestly strengthened the state's fiscal base, but sectarian power-sharing kept its autonomy shackled and its reach dependent on notables. Society remained moderately capable\u2014the 1952 elite-led ouster of Khuri showed real check on power\u2014yet za'im clientelism and rising sectarian/pan-Arab polarization channeled mobilization vertically rather than into cross-cutting civic institutions, leaving net society power roughly flat."}
